meaning: 卡尔巴拉[伊拉克中部城市](伊斯兰教什叶派的圣地)
examples: On April 14, a car bombing in Karbala killed 44 people and wounded scores more.
Abduredha Joda stood on a dry plot of land where he lives with his family on the outskirts of Karbala.
A train passenger traveling from Baghdad to Karbala, a Shiite holy city some 60 miles south of the capital, reads a prayer book.
Ibn Khaldun wrote his penetrating works on the philosophy of history some 700 years after Karbala.
In the south, Shiite rebels controlled major cities such as Karbala, Najaf, and Basra.
